content.query:
--Performing Bulk load into table using copy INTO function

CREATE TABLE bank_schema.customerdata(
    custid INT,
    fname varchar(100),
    lname varchar(100),
    age int,
    prof varchar(100)
)
WITH
(
     CLUSTERED COLUMNSTORE INDEX,
     DISTRIBUTION = ROUND_ROBIN
);


COPY INTO bank_schema.customerdata
FROM 'https://we47storage1.blob.core.windows.net/container1/sourcedir/custs'
WITH(
    FILE_TYPE = 'CSV',
    FIRSTROW = 1
);

select * from bank_schema.customerdata;


--Load the data from external storage account using Managed Identity, can be done on Dedicated warehouse also.

CREATE TABLE bank_schema.customerdata2(
    custid INT,
    fname varchar(100),
    lname varchar(100),
    age int,
    prof varchar(100)
)
WITH
(
     CLUSTERED COLUMNSTORE INDEX,
     DISTRIBUTION = ROUND_ROBIN
);



--Goto storage account -> IAM -> provide "Storage Blob data contributor" role for the synapse workspace managed identity

COPY INTO bank_schema.customerdata2
FROM 'https://izotherstorageacct.blob.core.windows.net/sourcecontainer1/source/custs'
WITH(
    FILE_TYPE = 'CSV',
    FIRSTROW = 1,
    CREDENTIAL=(IDENTITY='Managed Identity'));

select * from bank_schema.customerdata2;

--Last Layer for exposing data to business (Analytical Layer -> Semantic Layer)
--Expose Only Required Columns & rows to business creating views
--View is a stored query to reduce complexity of writing queries again and again or for applying column/row level restrictions for securing my data etc.,

--Materialized View Usecase
-- Customer Demographics Analytics Dashboard
--Slow without mat view as it scans full table every time
--create view viewname as 
--create materialized view viewname as 
SELECT
    prof,
    COUNT(*) AS total_customers,
    AVG(age) AS avg_age
FROM bank_schema.customerdata
GROUP BY prof;


--With Mat View (optimized)
-- Refreshes MV incrementally without any overhead
-- Avoids full table scan and avoid recomputation and it does only incremental refresh of the data from the base table (behind the scene)
-- Applicable in the analytical/gold layer more of, for best analytics performance (incremental, auto refresh, no need of writing complex queries)

--Materialized view (store current snapshot data, access only incremental data from the base table without doint full table scan, automatic refresh)
--Normal view (doesn't store data, access entire data every time from the entire base table, no automatic refresh)
CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW bank_schema.mv_customer_prof_stats1
WITH (DISTRIBUTION = HASH(prof))
AS
SELECT 
    prof,
    COUNT_BIG(*) AS total_customers,
    AVG(age) AS avg_age
    FROM bank_schema.customerdata
GROUP BY prof;

select * from bank_schema.mv_customer_prof_stats1;
--Was -> Athlete 196 9418 196 

insert into bank_schema.customerdata values(10002,'mohamed','irfan',44,'Athlete');
select * from bank_schema.mv_customer_prof_stats1;
--Now -> Athlete 197 9462 197 (behind the scene the mat view just added the one more record in the aggregation like... 196 + 1 added)
--If i go with normal view, it will take 1 + 1 + 1 +....196 + 1 = 197
